response.justification: The candidate's background is almost entirely in full-stack web and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), and C++ does not appear in the skills section at all — professional C++ use is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014-2015. This critically fails the 8-year C++ minimum requirement, which is the primary technical pillar of the role. Beyond the language gap, the resume shows no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, hardware or storage architecture, or sustained large-scale infrastructure work, and any leadership experience is informal rather than organizational.
strengths: Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O patterns
Consistent commitment to engineering best practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied across multiple roles
Community knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects demonstrates a genuine mentoring orientation
concerns: C++ is absent from the skills section; the only professional C++ work spans roughly six months, making the 8-year minimum qualification unmet by a wide margin
No evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, infrastructure architecture, or work at the hardware, storage, or compute layer
Technical leadership experience is informal (dojos, workshops, open-source) rather than demonstrated team or organizational leadership of cloud or DevOps engineers
